description_long: This object is a small fig plant branch with several broad, lobed green leaves and a few unripe green figs attached to a short, woody stem. The leaves display a mix of healthy green and some areas with dark spots, suggesting natural wear or minor disease. The figs are round, smooth, and green, indicating they are not yet ripe. The stem is relatively short and appears freshly cut, with no visible pot or soil, giving the impression of a branch or cutting rather than a full potted plant. The overall structure is lightweight and delicate, with the leaves spreading outward and the fruits clustered near the center. This type of plant is commonly found in urban gardens, patios, or as part of decorative arrangements.
description: A small fig plant branch with broad green leaves and several unripe green figs attached to a short woody stem. The leaves show some natural spotting and the overall form is airy and delicate.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cluster of green figs and the spread of large, lobed leaves with visible spotting.
description_view_1: Back view dominated by the broad leaves, partially obscuring the figs and stem.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the curvature of the stem and the arrangement of leaves and figs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clearer look at the underside of the leaves and a single fig more prominently visible.
